_comment: Soundtrack manifest. Drop audio files (mp3/ogg/m4a) into this music/ folder named EXACTLY as each 'file' below, and they auto-load in the in-game player. The FIRST entry is the opening theme. 'artist' and 'note' are documentation only — the player shows 'title'. The audio itself must be added by you (copyright); these are the recommended tracks.

tracks.title: La Maldicion de Malinche — Amparo Ochoa
tracks.note: OPENING THEME. The betrayed, unfinished nation — the thesis of the Rivera prologue.

tracks.title: Me gustan los estudiantes — Violeta Parra
tracks.note: The 1968 student movement; the summer, the brigades.

tracks.title: Tlatelolco — Judith Reyes
tracks.note: The corrido of October 2. The movement's own documentary songwriter.

tracks.title: A desalambrar — Daniel Viglietti
tracks.note: Tear down the fences — the campesino land struggle, the toma de tierras.

tracks.title: El Barzon — Amparo Ochoa
tracks.note: The peasant's debt and dignity; the countryside.

tracks.title: Cuando tenga la tierra — Mercedes Sosa
tracks.note: The agrarian dream; the land promised in 1917.

tracks.title: Te recuerdo Amanda — Victor Jara
tracks.note: The worker and the factory gate; tenderness under repression.

tracks.title: Manifiesto — Victor Jara
tracks.note: Why one sings. Resonates with the 1976 Chile-style intervention ending.

tracks.title: Solo le pido a Dios — Mercedes Sosa
tracks.note: The dirty war, the memorial, the dead in the game treated accordingly.

tracks.title: Gracias a la vida — Mercedes Sosa
tracks.note: An elegiac note for the finale / the Rivera coda.

tracks.title: Corrido de Lucio Cabanas
tracks.note: The armed road, the sierra of Guerrero, the schoolteacher who went up the mountain.

tracks.title: Venceremos — Inti-Illimani
tracks.note: The offensive, the grand operation, the auge armada.

tracks.title: Metro Balderas — Rockdrigo Gonzalez
tracks.note: 1980s urban Mexico City; the colonos, the periphery, the bard who died in the 1985 quake.

tracks.title: La era esta pariendo un corazon — Silvio Rodriguez
tracks.note: The long accumulation; a new era being born, painfully.

tracks.title: Yo pisare las calles nuevamente — Pablo Milanes
tracks.note: Return and vindication after the dictatorship; hope deferred, not dead.

tracks.title: El pueblo unido jamas sera vencido — Quilapayun
tracks.note: The 1988 mobilization, the Zocalo filled, the reconocimiento ending. (Pointed irony given the PCM's condemnation of Prague.)
